purpose: AgentEchelon is a governed, multi-party, multi-channel AI platform you deploy into your own AWS account. This context lets the assistant answer questions about the platform itself - what it is, how it works, and where a topic is documented.
overview: **AgentEchelon is a governed, multi-party agentic AI platform that runs entirely in your own AWS account and serves internal and customer-facing use cases from one place.** A core feature is that agent governance can be enforced with the cloud's own primitives instead of a bolted-on policy engine. Every user, assistant, and conversation is an AWS resource with an ARN; every actor holds a bearer-pi
answering_guidance: Answer from these summaries and, when deployed in Aurora mode, from the retrieved full docs. Point the user at the named doc path for detail. Do not fabricate; if it is not here, say so.

docs.title: Agent Echelon
docs.summary: **Agent Echelon is a self-hosted agent control plane: a governed, multi-party agentic AI platform that runs entirely in your own AWS account and serves your internal and customer-facing use cases from one place.** It is the governed layer between your agents and everything they touch (the harness around your models), deciding who may act, which model answers, where the context lives, and what gets

docs.title: SPEC: Context-aware model routing (RoutingContext + provider adapter)
docs.summary: **Status:** Partial (`RoutingContext` runs on the standard profile; the provider-adapter seam is design)

docs.title: Demo Company Spec: Stratum Technologies
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (the demo dataset and seed script ship)

docs.title: Federated Participants - bring external people in, safely, from any identity provider
docs.summary: **Status:** Design (a wired, opt-in seam; not the shipped experience)

docs.title: Admin Console Guide
docs.summary: How operators use the AgentEchelon admin dashboard to check platform health, review how the assistants are performing, and administer conversations. Written to be portable - it describes behaviour, not any specific AWS account.

docs.title: Bringing Your Own Admin Console & Admin Auth
docs.summary: This guide explains how to run AgentEchelon's operator surface - analytics, administration, evaluations, experiments - **behind your own application's admin console and admin authentication**, instead of AgentEchelon's built-in Cognito `admins` group and React dashboard.

docs.title: Aurora Mode Guide
docs.summary: AgentEchelon ships with two analytics backends. **Athena mode** (default) is serverless and cheap at low volume. **Aurora mode** adds a VPC with Aurora PostgreSQL Serverless v2 for sub-second dashboard queries, multi-turn evaluation, drift detection, and cross-conversation context search.

docs.title: A/B Testing and Running Battles
docs.summary: How to run real A/B model experiments on your own traffic, measure which model wins, promote the winner, and use Battle Mode to put two assistants head to head on the same prompt.

docs.title: Image Generation Providers
docs.summary: **Audience:** Deployers configuring `/battle` generation-out (and any future feature that calls `invokeImageGenModel`).

docs.title: Infrastructure and cost model
docs.summary: **Audience:** deployers and operators sizing an AgentEchelon instance, and anyone reviewing what each piece of infrastructure is for and what it costs to run.

docs.title: Resource Tagging & Cost Attribution
docs.summary: This platform deploys as **multiple independent instances** (one per `AE_INSTANCE_NAME`). For cost attribution to work, each instance's resources must carry **its own** identity - so the tagging rule is:

docs.title: Amazon Chime SDK Integration
docs.summary: The frontend talks to AWS through Amazon Chime SDK Messaging: users authenticate with Cognito, exchange their identity for temporary AWS credentials, and use those credentials to create channels, send messages, and receive real-time updates. Assistant responses are generated by AWS Bedrock and delivered back into the channel as persistent messages.

docs.title: AgentEchelon Design System
docs.summary: The design tokens and shared component classes described here are implemented in `frontend/packages/shared/src/styles/`. Some component CSS is still being migrated onto them - the [Migration Guide](#migration-guide) below is the reference for bringing a component onto the system.

docs.title: Developer Guide
docs.summary: **Audience:** engineers building on or extending AgentEchelon. **Status:** hub / index. Points to the authoritative doc for each task; it does not restate them.

docs.title: Guide: Building and operating assistant context
docs.summary: **Audience:** developers building an assistant for a new use case, and admins/deployers who load and scope the content an assistant reads.

docs.title: How to add or manage an assistant profile
docs.summary: A practical guide for two adjacent tasks the capability-profiles model ([SPEC-PER-PROFILE-OWNERSHIP.md](../../specs/assistant-context/SPEC-PER-PROFILE-OWNERSHIP.md)) makes routine:

docs.title: Latency targets (industry-sourced)
docs.summary: The admin console's latency targets (`frontend/packages/admin/src/components/admin/metricTargets.ts` and `LatencyTab.latencyColor`) are set to published industry standards, not to a self-imposed ceiling. This doc records the standards and how they map onto AgentEchelon so the numbers are defensible and reviewable.

docs.title: Message delivery & size: how assistant output reaches Amazon Chime SDK
docs.summary: Canonical implementation: `backend/lambda/src/lib/async-processor-core.ts`.

docs.title: Message flow - how a message travels, who touches it, and why
docs.summary: AgentEchelon is a **multiparty** substrate: humans and assistants share Amazon Chime SDK channels. The flow below is what lets a channel be a plain human-to-human room, a 1:1 human↔assistant chat, or a mixed room where the assistant answers only when addressed - all with the same primitives, differing only in **bot configuration**

docs.title: Metadata and Tags: What Goes Where
docs.summary: This guide is the rule for storing data on Amazon Chime SDK channels and users, and for using resource tags. It exists because the wrong choice leaks information to other users or silently weakens the tier boundary. Read it before you add a field to a channel, a user, or a tag.

docs.title: Model Strategy
docs.summary: Agent Echelon treats model routing as a capability-first concern, not a tier-only choice.

docs.title: RAG - Document Retrieval
docs.summary: **Audience:** deployers who want the agent to ground its responses in project documentation (wiki pages, runbooks, design docs) rather than just the LLM's training data.

docs.title: npm Supply-Chain Hardening
docs.summary: This project treats every npm dependency as untrusted code that runs on your machine and in CI. This document records the controls the repo ships, why they exist, and how to work with them.

docs.title: Demo setup and post-deploy validation
docs.summary: A runbook for standing up the **Stratum Technologies** demo and running the end-to-end validation that exercises every tier flow with real (measured, recorded) conversations, then verifies the admin dashboard. This is the POC surface: read it to understand how the platform behaves, and modify the demo data / tests for your own use case. For the demo company design, see

docs.title: Deploying the frontend (CloudFront + S3)
docs.summary: The AgentEchelon SPA deploys to **CloudFront + private S3 by default**. The `AgentEchelonFrontend` stack (part of `cdk deploy --all`) creates the hosting; a short build-and-publish step uploads the app. Local development still uses the Vite dev server (`npm run dev`) - this guide is for a real, shareable deploy.

docs.title: Integrating Your Identity Provider
docs.summary: This guide explains how to connect your existing identity provider (IdP) to AgentEchelon so your users can authenticate with your organization's SSO, LDAP, or OAuth2 system instead of (or in addition to) Cognito User Pool username/password.

docs.title: AgentEchelon Troubleshooting Guide
docs.summary: All `aws` examples assume credentials for **your target account** are already configured (via `AWS_PROFILE`, environment variables, SSO, or an instance role). Examples omit `--profile`; export `AWS_PROFILE=<your-profile>` (or set creds) for the shell first. Confirm with:

docs.title: Architecture Guide
docs.summary: This document explains how AgentEchelon works end-to-end. It covers every major flow, how the CDK stacks connect, and where to look when extending or debugging the system.

docs.title: AgentEchelon Overview
docs.summary: **AgentEchelon is a governed, multi-party agentic AI platform that runs entirely in your own AWS account and serves internal and customer-facing use cases from one place.** A core feature is that agent governance can be enforced with the cloud's own primitives instead of a bolted-on policy engine. Every user, assistant, and conversation is an AWS resource with an ARN; every actor holds a bearer-pi

docs.title: AgentEchelon Tenets
docs.summary: The non-negotiables. Every architectural decision in the project traces back to one of these, and where two designs competed, the tenet broke the tie.

docs.title: AgentEchelon documentation
docs.summary: The navigable map of everything under `docs/`, organized by **what you are trying to do**. The authoritative project overview and quickstart live in the root [`README.md`](../README.md); start there if you have not.

docs.title: SPEC - Admin Console (design)
docs.summary: **Status:** Design (the admin-console design; for using it, see the admin guide)

docs.title: SPEC: Separate Admin Console App
docs.summary: **Status:** P0-P3 BUILT. P0-P1: the standalone admin app + the chat-app cutover. P2: the frontend is an npm-workspaces monorepo (`@ae/shared`, `@ae/chat`, `@ae/admin`) - shared code (types, apiCall with an injectable token provider, AuthProvider, the reused credential-exchange client, i18n) factored into `@ae/shared`, `chimeService` split into chat messaging (`@ae/chat`) + admin actions (`@ae/admi

docs.title: SPEC: Abuse Controls (rate limiting, spend budgets, request dedup)
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (all phases). `lib/abuse-controls.ts` holds the logic, wired into all three tiers via `agent-classification-common.abuseControlsWiring`: request dedup (guard in `runSharedPipeline` + stable `correlationId` from `CHIME.message.id`), per-user/global spend budget with a fail-safe global ceiling, an SSM circuit trip, a per-tier hourly rate limit, and an inbound length cap. Acti

docs.title: SPEC: Optional Aurora PostgreSQL + VPC Deployment Mode
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (opt-in deployment mode)

docs.title: SPEC: Cost Sleep Mode (auto-sleep / wake)
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (Aurora-mode, opt-in `-c sleepMode=true`). Wired in `bin/backend.ts` (reads the `sleepMode` / `sleepAfterIdle` / `sleepCheckRate` / `sleepRecipients` context), provisioned by `analytics-stack-aurora.ts`, with the runtime in `lambda/src/deployment-sleep.ts` + `lib/sleep-mode.ts`. **Scope:** A deploy-time opt-in that auto-pauses the Aurora data plane after a configurable idle

docs.title: SPEC: Drift Detection
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (Aurora mode; the live-suggestion path is behind `enableLiveDrift`)

docs.title: Assistant Configuration - what the assistant *is*, per experience
docs.summary: **Status:** Partial (the per-tier config seams ship; the unified `AssistantConfig` bundle is the design target)

docs.title: SPEC: Bilingual conversations (reply-language, pivot, dual delivery)
docs.summary: **Status:** Partial (reply-language ships; the inference pivot and dual delivery are design)

docs.title: SPEC: Configurable intent pack (per-deployment intent taxonomy)
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (the taxonomy mechanism; the domain taxonomy is per-deployment config)

docs.title: SPEC: Per-profile ownership (`AgentEchelonClassification-*`)
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented

docs.title: SPEC: User profile store and once-per-user onboarding
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (pending live deploy + e2e validation)

docs.title: Cross-Channel Task Continuity
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented

docs.title: SPEC: Conversation Archive and Member Removal (moderator)
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented. Archive mechanism per [ADR-017](../../design/decisions/017-conversation-archive-mechanism.md) - Option 1 (mark archived + read-only via an immutable tag), composed with the conversation-type 90-day Amazon Chime SDK expiration TTL. This spec details the behavior and the identity/credential model.

docs.title: Conversation Configuration - a conversation is a configurable experience
docs.summary: **Status:** Partial (the `classification` type seam and the dimension/connector schemas ship and default to the tier today; the full conversation-type flexibility is the design target)

docs.title: The AgentEchelon Interaction Layer - platform composition model
docs.summary: **Audience:** anyone deciding *whether* AgentEchelon fits their use case and gives them the right level of security, plus contributors who need to see how the interaction layer and security model is built before diving into a new use case or editing an existing use case. (Deployers who want *how do I add or edit one* want the **guides** in §7; this doc is the model behind them.)

docs.title: SPEC: Compact message metadata - a coded codebook + out-of-band lookup
docs.summary: **Status:** Partial (out-of-band lookup and the cap-shedding backstop ship; the coded-state codebook is design)

docs.title: SPEC: Conversation ↔ transport notification bridge (email now; SMS + voice/PSTN next)
docs.summary: The **conversation channel is the hub**; each transport is bridged to it, both directions, so the assistant is transport-agnostic.

docs.title: SPEC: Welcome flow and assistant context
docs.summary: **Status:** Partial (the welcome wiring ships; later context phases are design)

docs.title: SPEC: `/battle` - Multi-Assistant Adversarial Reply
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (premium-gated). Covers single-turn, `TASK_*`, and image (vision-in + generation-out) battles, the three-axis scorecard, and per-step telemetry. **Image-output safety:** ships as a basic default; production-grade moderation is the deployer's responsibility (open-source posture), not an internal sign-off gate.

docs.title: Access control by example: blocked interactions, the policy that blocks them, and the experience
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (reference: worked examples of the live IAM enforcement)

docs.title: Identity & Access Model
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (reference: the live identity and access model)

docs.title: Access & Controls Auditing - an append-only record of who could act, and what changed
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (audit capture and the membership-history view; forward extensions noted inline)

docs.title: SPEC: Add a user to a conversation (eligible-member invite and assistant escalation)
docs.summary: Adding another human to a conversation should be safe by construction: the person added must be entitled to the conversation's tier, and no one should be able to email an arbitrary address or pull in someone who cannot actually access the thread. AgentEchelon supports two ways to add a user, and both pass through the SAME

docs.title: Making admin-console actions IAM-enforceable
docs.summary: **Status:** Built, flag-gated (opt-in), pending deploy validation. The enforcement is off by default; `-c adminIamEnforcement=true` (backend) plus `VITE_ADMIN_IAM_ENFORCEMENT=true` (admin app) turns it on, and the interim Cognito-group gate (`callerCanReadArchive`) stays as the `ae-cognito` fallback. The example personas (section 2) are opt-in behind `-c enableAdminPersonas=true`. What is wired: t

docs.title: SPEC: Admin Agent and Admin Notification Channel
docs.summary: **Status:** DESIGN (not yet built). This spec supersedes the initial admin-notification-channel attempt (the stack at `backend/lib/stacks/admin-notification-stack.ts` and its handler `backend/lambda/src/admin-notification-channel-provision.ts`), which created and posted to the channel as the service app-instance-admin. That is wrong (see Why) and is being reworked to the model below.

docs.title: SPEC: Admin Identity
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented

docs.title: Conversation Security, Membership, and Information Isolation
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented (Layers 1-6 are live; Layer 7 mixed-trust visibility is partial)

docs.title: Identity & Access - anyone participates at the right capability, only as themselves
docs.summary: **Status:** Implemented. The **Identity & Access** pillar of the interaction layer (`docs/specs/conversation-messaging/SPEC-INTERACTION-LAYER.md` is the map), built as a backend **Credential Exchange Service** (see §9).

docs.title: SPEC - Content Moderation Model (surfaces)
